title: Microcoelia macrorrhynchia

This genus, Microcoelia, is further grouped within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Microcoelia macrorrhynchia is a parasitic orchid, meaning it obtains its nutrients by attaching to and drawing them from a host plant, typically a species of epiphyte. As a parasitic orchid, Microcoelia macrorrhynchia is often found in tropical regions where the humidity and temperature are suitable for its growth. Further classification of Microcoelia macrorrhynchia may also involve its placement within the tribe or tribe-like structure within Orchidaceae.
